title: centaurea raphanina subsp. mixta
intro: Centaurea raphanina subsp. mixta, also known as the Mixed Broom Cornflower, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly due to its high concentration of saponins and flavonoids, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Centaurea raphanina subsp. mixta include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support immune function, making it a valuable addition to herbal remedies. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
